title: Delhi HC refuses to immediately restore Cockroach Janta Party’s X account in India
content: A Bench of Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav issued notice to Centre and directed Mr. Dipke to approach the review committee set up under the IT Rule.

title: Internal bickering, resignations and public anger, Trinamool battling on multiple fronts
content: The differences between two senior MPs of the party, Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar and Kalyan Banerjee, became public. Trinamool MP Sougata Roy faced protests, and eggs were hurled at his car

title: Fishermen seek infrastructure, training to revive tuna fishing harbour in Tiruvottiyur
content: If the tuna fishing harbour constructed at a cost of ₹200 crore at Tiruvottiyur Kuppam is to be put to use, fishermen must have the skills to catch quality tuna suitable for export, said M.D. Dayalan of the Indian Fishermen Association

title: SC sets three-month deadline on High Courts to pronounce judgment in a case
content: The Bench held that the bail orders must be ideally pronounced the next day, and communicated to the jail the same day

title: Karnataka government formation LIVE: Day after resigning as CM, Siddaramaiah meets Rahul Gandhi in Delhi
content: Siddaramaiah thanked Rahul Gandhi for giving him an opportunity to serve the State and informed him that he had resigned from the Chief Minister’s post as asked

title: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 'extreme moral cowardice' for standing as Israel's strongest supporter: Congress
content: Prime Minister Modi never condemned the targeted assassination of the Iranian head of state, never condemned the ongoing Israeli genocide in Gaza, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: After Karnataka, Congress signals it has the appetite to settle Chief Ministerial feuds
content: Congress leaders Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi’s success in persuading Siddaramaiah to step down may now provide the party a template to handle similar leadership disputes

title: NHAI identifies 1600 kms for monetisation for FY 27
content: The identified projects are across Haryana, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Maharashtra.

title: Part of under-construction bridge collapses in Uttar Pradesh during storm; six killed, 2 feared trapped
content: Local residents said construction work was underway when a severe storm hit the area. Soon afterwards, portions of the bridge collapsed, bringing down supporting pillars and trapping workers under the debris

title: India to see 90% of long period average rainfall this monsoon: IMD
content: The monsoon which was forecast to arrive in Kerala on May 26 would be likely in the first week of June, the IMD said

title: Karnataka Governor accepts resignation; Shivakumar to succeed, Siddaramaiah reaches 10, Janpath in Delhi
content: Mr. Siddaramaiah will be meeting with the Congress top brass today to discuss the formation of the next government and the next president of the Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee

title: India, Canada launch trade and investment forum to strengthen economic partnership
content: Canada-India Trade and Investment Forum launched for enhancing economic ties and fostering business partnerships between the two countries

title: Kerala Assembly session highlights: Governor delivers policy address, proposes White Paper on State's finances
content: Governor Rajendra Arlekar's policy address focused on welfare and development measures, including a new water policy, menstrual leave for school students, strengthening Responsible Tourism and seeking support for its proposals in the Assembly and among the public

title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: Supreme Court
content: A Bench of Justices J.K. Maheshwari and A.S. Chandurkar also directed the States to furnish periodic compliance reports by organising monthly meetings and uploading the minutes on the concerned portals

title: Tussle for the top spot in Karnataka: A timeline
content: A mass leader with strong rural connect, Siddaramaiah’s second stint as CM of Karnataka was marked by controversies and internal dissidence.

title: A ‘Work-Live-Play’ township vs. farmers
content: 9,640 acres of land spread across nine revenue and 17 non-revenue villages has been identified for developing the Greater Bengaluru Integrated Township Project, popularly known as the Bidadi Township Project. But are farmers on board? Chetan B.C. and Aditya Bharadwaj find out.

title: The Leiden copper plates, recently returned to India, are a vital record of Chola history
content: Not mere inscriptions, scholars say, the plates bear living evidence of Chola administration, land grants and maritime links.

title: Counting of votes for Municipal elections under way in Punjab
content: A total of 7,554 candidates are in the fray for the local body elections, seen as crucial for all the major political parties in Punjab – AAP, BJP, Congress and SAD – in the run-up to next year’s Assembly polls

title: Vijayapura-based BLDE Deemed University signs pact with US varsity for student exchange programme
content: The agreement allows students of the two institutions to choose elective subjects to study in each other’s campus

title: Space Wrap: A blockbuster month for India’s private sector
content: This article is part of Space Wrap, a round-up of space news and developments in and related to India every month

title: People cautioned against depositing money with Belagavi-based Shivam Associates
content: Yadgir Superintendent of Police Pruthvik Shankar said that a case has been registered against the company on the charge of alleged fraudulent transactions with public money

title: Candid conversations on Kashmir expected at The Hindu Huddle
content: J&K CM Omar Abdullah will be engaged in candid dialogue with Dr. Narayan Lakshman, Opinion Editor, The Hindu, and Curator, The Hindu Huddle, in a session titled ‘Beyond the Valley: The role of Kashmir in great power politics’ on June 5

title: Siddaramaiah and D.K. Shivakumar: A public bonhomie that never let up
content: On May 28, when Mr. Siddaramaiah eventually resigned, Mr. Shivakumar was seen touching his feet and both were clicked in a warm embrace

title: After smooth transition, new Karnataka CM has a delicate task ahead
content: One of the biggest challenges before the new Chief Minister would be balancing his relationship with Siddaramaiah’s loyalists and support base within the Congress

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | Why football’s most passionate fans may be in India
content: In Kerala, West Bengal and several other places across India, the FIFA World Cup is a homecoming — of fan clubs, midnight screenings, bike rallies and WhatsApp groups that spring back to life every four years

title: Child trafficking gangs on prowl, target poverty-stricken women in Andhra
content: Child trafficking and baby-selling gangs are on the prowl in Andhra Pradesh, taking advantage of destitute families, who are willing to sell their babies due to poverty. Rajulapudi Srinivas reports about child trafficking gangs having widespread network in States like Gujarat, Maharashtra, Delhi, and Telangana

title: National Health Accounts figures indicate high burden of health care costs on people
content: As per the latest figures from the National Health Accounts Estimates for India 2022-23, out of pocket expenditure is nearly half of the current health expenditure. Experts flag continued high level of unregulated privatisation, leading to major inequities in access to healthcare.

title: Rape convict Asaram surrenders at Jodhpur jail after High Court upholds life sentence
content: Though Asaram’s life imprisonment sentence was upheld, the court acquitted him of charges related to gang rape and gang penetrative sexual assault of the minor girl child under the IPC and POCSO Act

title: Heatwave warning issued for Telangana as temperatures set to soar above 44°C
content: The forecast stated that Adilabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem and Khammam are expected to record temperatures above 44°C on May 29, while Kumram Bheem Asifabad and Mancherial are also likely to experience similar conditions on both May 29 and May 30

title: BJP demands Uniform Civil Code implemented in Telangana
content: Recent unseasonal rains have left heaps of paddy, maize, and sunflower crops soaked and sprouting at the centers, yet the government has failed to respond

title: KIA pips Mumbai to become second busiest domestic airport in the country
content: In the international sector, CSMIA retained the second spot as it handled 11,53,949 passengers and KIA handled 5,06,309 passengers

title: Id celebrated with religious fervour in Uttar Pradesh amid tight security
content: The devotees gathered in mosques and Idgahs for special prayers as administrations across the State maintained strict vigil and appealed to maintain peace and brotherhood

title: Indians trapped in Myanmar’s fraudulent digital scam centre seeks immediate rescue, repatriation, alleges extreme mental trauma
content: In response to the organisation’s outreach and petitions, the Embassy of India in Yangon on May 21 replied that the matter is being followed up vigorously with the concerned Union, State and local Myanmar authorities for rescue and repatriation of the Indian nationals

title: West Bengal government faces Annapurna challenge, to verify beneficiaries before rolling out cash transfer scheme
content: The financial burden which Annapurna Bhandar would have on the State exchequer is what is prompting the West Bengal government to verify the existing beneficiaries of Lakshmir Bhandar and drag its feet on the implementation of the scheme

title: No ‘double standards’ in fight against terrorism, says NSA Ajit Doval in Russia
content: The National Security Adviser participated in the first International Security Forum and the 14th Meeting of High Representatives for Security Matters in Moscow

title: No legal action if infiltrators go back on their own: Amit Shah
content: Union government committed to identifying and deporting every single infiltrator from the country, says Home Minister

title: First Kuki village in Churachandpur resettled 3 years after conflict began
content: Only the males have returned to Khodang, a village near the “buffer zone”, almost where three districts meet

title: Supreme Court tells CCI to protect competition without weakening market confidence; clears Amazon
content: The court concluded that the CCI acted outside its statutory authority by withdrawing the approval given to the Amazon-Future Coupons combination in 2019

title: Gujarat’s Banni grasslands: A heap of broken images, where the sun beats
content: NTPC’s proposed solar project in Kachchh’s Banni grassland is pitting India’s renewable energy ambitions against one of Asia’s most fragile ecosystems and the Maldharis, who have depended on the land for centuries. Abhinay Deshpande reports on the area that holds the Chhari-Dhand wetland conservation reserve and a hill that is both a refuge and compass for the pastoralists

title: Indian Air Force likely to oversee transport of NEET-UG re-test papers
content: This measure follows the cancellation of the original examination after an alleged question paper leak

title: A heat crisis in Telangana that burns beyond the thermometer
content: As Telangana battles one of its harshest summers in recent years, the rising burden of suspected heatstroke deaths and worsening urban heat stress are exposing the growing human cost of extreme weather. Even as the State rolls out heat mitigation measures and advisories, questions remain over ground-level implementation and the true scale of heat-related fatalities. Siddharth Kumar Singh reports on how the extreme heat is reshaping life, labour and survival

title: Watch: Karnataka CM change: Siddaramaiah resigns, what’s next? | Above the Fold | 28.05.2026
content: From Siddaramaiah’s resignation and the Congress leadership transition in Karnataka to India-China talks over the LAC and the Madras High Court’s cow slaughter ban ahead of Bakrid, and more.

title: Pune Court frames charges in Nasrapur child murder case in 28 days
content: The accused’s lawyers opposed the framing of charges during his first production. The court, after verifying the evidence, framed charges against him

title: Chhattisgarh BJP MLA accused of assaulting government official
content: The Nayab Tehsildar alleged that Ramkumar Toppo, who represents Sitapur constituency, assaulted him on May 27 after accusing him of misbehaving with his relative

title: HC issues notice to Centre, X on plea against blocking of Cockroach Janta Party account
content: The Delhi High Court has issued notices to the Union government and social media platform X regarding the suspension of the Cockroach Janta Party's account. The party's X handle gained viral attention following a campaign linked to a controversial remark by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, who later clarified his statement.

title: SC invokes Article 142, issues directions to high courts to prevent delays in judgments
content: The Supreme Court has issued binding directions to all high courts to expedite judgment delivery and reduce judicial delays. Reserved judgments must now be pronounced within three months, with bail orders to be delivered on the same or next day. All judgments will be uploaded online within 24 hours of pronouncement.

title: Jasprit Bumrah next Mumbai Indians captain for IPL 2027?
content: Following a dismal IPL 2026, Mumbai Indians face intense captaincy speculation. Fans overwhelmingly favor pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah to lead in 2027, with Rohit Sharma also a strong contender. Bumrah's successful interim captaincy stint and the team's continued title drought fuel the debate for a leadership revival.

title: Relief for seer Avimukteshwaranand: SC upholds pre-arrest bail in POCSO case
content: The Allahabad high court in March granted anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati and his disciple Mukundanand Brahmachari in a POCSO case alleging sexual exploitation of minor disciples. Justice Jitendra Kumar Sinha, while allowing their plea, directed both the accused and the complainant, Ashutosh Maharaj, not to speak to the media about the case. The court had earlier stayed Avimukteshwaranand's arrest on February 27 and reserved its verdict after directing the applicants to cooperate with the investigation.

title: Amazon shuts down 'employee AI leadership board' & the reason is rising costs
content: Amazon has reportedly deactivated an internal AI usage leaderboard after employees "tokenmaxxing" to inflate their scores, leading to increased computing costs. The move highlights growing concerns for tech companies about rising AI expenses, as Amazon shifts focus to "normalised deployments" measuring useful code creation over raw token consumption.

title: Beyond Gayle, beyond Tendulkar: Sooryavanshi so extraordinary that Moody turns to Bradman
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is electrifying IPL 2026, shattering records and drawing comparisons to cricket legends. He's already surpassed Chris Gayle's sixes record and is being likened to Sachin Tendulkar for his composure. Former Australian coach Tom Moody even invoked Don Bradman, highlighting Sooryavanshi's unprecedented dominance in T20 cricket at such a young age.

title: Air India plane that stayed airborne for 9hrs was under repair for 6 years
content: An Air India flight to San Francisco returned to Delhi after nine hours due to a malfunctioning Traffic Collision Avoidance System. The Boeing 777, named 'Goa', had been grounded for six years at Nagpur's MRO facility due to a shortage of spare parts. Critical components, including engines and landing gear, were replaced during its extensive overhaul.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: Down the rabbit hole: Bangladeshi buffalo 'Trump' pardoned – brief history of animal sacrifices
content: We live in the era of viral animals, from Larry the Cat, the permanent fixture at 10 Downing Street, to Moo Deng, the cute pygmy hippo in Thailand, and Punch, the lonely monkey whose inability to make friends made the world go aww. Donald Trump the buffalo might be the latest addition to this pantheon. But long before the algorithm turned animals into celebrities, humans were already turning them into gods, omens, sacrifices, scapegoats and pardoned prisoners.

title: Karnataka 'nataka' enters a new phase? Siddaramaiah meets top brass; cabinet berths on radar
content: A day after annoucing that he would step down as chief miniter to pave way for Shivakumar in accordance with the rotational formula agreed upon after the 2023 win, Siddaramaiah expressed his desire to stay in Benglauru than move to Rajya Sabha which was part of an offer pitched by the party high command. Siddaramaiah's move to "partly  agree" with the top brass, while holding his ground on not moving to the Rajdhaani for national politics largely suggest that the deveopment may mutate to become a complex quagmire.

title: Back in Tajpur, Sooryavanshi remains the kid who never forgives or forgets
content: While the cricket world marvelled at Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s breathtaking 29-ball 97 in the IPL Eliminator, people in his village of Tajpur watched it like just another day. From smashing bowlers twice his age as a teenager to rapidly making adjustments within weeks, the 15-year-old’s rise has stunned even seasoned cricketers but not the clan that saw him take his early steps in Bihar.

title: Peter Thiel has an 'escape plan' from America, the same as Google's founders
content: Peter Thiel, billionaire Trump donor and Palantir co-founder, has quietly relocated to Argentina, eyeing it as a backup country. Thiel bought a Buenos Aires mansion, enrolled his kids in school, and bonded with President Javier Milei. His move mirrors Google founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin's California exit, all driven by a proposed 5 percent wealth tax targeting the state's billionaires. Here's what's happening.

title: Watch: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes in a massive fireball while undergoing a test
content: In a dramatic turn of events, Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket was engulfed in flames during a launch pad catastrophe at Cape Canaveral. Fortunately, founder Jeff Bezos announced that all team members are safe, with an investigation already underway.

title: Salman Khan acts as peacemaker in Ranveer Singh and Farhan Akhtar's dispute over 'Don 3'
content: Salman Khan is reportedly stepping in to mediate a brewing 'Don 3' dispute between Ranveer Singh and Farhan Akhtar, stemming from creative differences.  The superstar has personally contacted both to foster an amicable resolution, urging them to prioritize future projects and industry unity.  Both actors are said to be taking his advice seriously, aiming for a private settlement. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Claude Lemieux death rumors explained: What really happened to NHL legend?
content: Former NHL player Claude Lemieux has died at the age of 60. TMZ Sports reported that he may have died by suicide, though no official confirmation has been released publicly. Lemieux won four Stanley Cups during his legendary NHL career and became known as one of hockey’s fiercest playoff competitors. Fans are also remembering his emotional final appearance in Montreal earlier this week.

title: Blake Lively hits breaking point amid Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce fallout rumors: Report
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have remained distant and private about their feelings for the Hollywood power couple, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, as they gear up to get married in the next few weeks in New York City. As speculations swirl about whether Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds will be attending Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s high profile wedding, it seems the actress has reached a breaking point in her life.

title: Claude Lemieux family post takes heartbreaking turn after emotional message resurfaces
content: Claude Lemieux’s death at age 60 has left the hockey world heartbroken. TMZ Sports and The Athletic reported that the NHL legend died by suicide. Fans also revisited an emotional old post from his son Brendan Lemieux after the news surfaced. The four-time Stanley Cup champion is being remembered for his legendary playoff career, fearless style, and unforgettable moments in NHL history.

title: Burnt Scorpio, diesel traces: Ex-Congress sarpanch family’s quadruple murder shocks Rajasthan
content: A charred SUV found with four bodies in Ajmer district has shifted focus from accident to suspected murder. Police discovered bloodstains and evidence at the family's residence, suggesting a planned killing before the vehicle was set ablaze. Disturbing details about domestic violence have also surfaced as the investigation continues.

title: 'Crazy love': Netanyahu cites India's support even as world 'delegitimises' Israel
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed gratitude for India's "crazy love" and unwavering support, highlighting it as a stark contrast to global efforts to "delegitimize" the Jewish state. He noted that India stands with Israel when much of the world keeps it at a distance, emphasizing their unique relationship.

title: IPL’s explosive media rights growth unmatched in global sport: Dhumal
content: IPL governing council chairman Arun Dhumal said the league’s media rights growth is unmatched in global sport, highlighting its massive return on investment in just 18 years. He credited fan engagement, close contests and the IPL’s talent pipeline for India’s cricketing dominance and growing commercial value

title: Filmmaker Anik Dutta died by suicide, say cops
content: Kolkata police have concluded that filmmaker Anik Dutta's death was a suicide. Investigations revealed a note, slippers on the roof, and CCTV footage showing Dutta proceeding directly to the rooftop. The postmortem report confirms injuries consistent with a fall from a significant height, solidifying the suicide theory.

title: Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie's son files legal documents to drop 'Pitt' surname
content: Maddox, son of Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt, has reportedly sought to legally remove 'Pitt' from his surname, becoming the fourth of their children to do so amid their ongoing public feud. This move follows his professional credit as 'Maddox Jolie' in a recent film. The family's strained relations are reportedly a significant factor in these surname changes.

title: Flight attendant’s picture with Tamil Nadu CM Vijay mid-flight sends fans into complete meltdown
content: A viral in-flight photo of Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has thrilled fans, capturing a memorable moment for a flight attendant. This comes as Vijay makes his first official visit to New Delhi, meeting Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

title: Forms of mantra chanting: How each type shapes mind and energy
content: Mantra chanting transcends mere repetition, with distinct methods profoundly altering the experience and mental impact. From loud Vaikhari Japa for atmosphere to inward-focused Upanshu and mental Manasik Japa, each form offers unique benefits. Likhit Japa grounds restless minds, while Sankirtan fosters collective energy. Advanced Ajapa Japa sees the mantra flow effortlessly, signifying a deep spiritual connection.

title: Retired IAS officer dies after AC blast triggers fire at south Delhi home
content: A tragic fire, reportedly sparked by an AC blast, claimed the life of an 80-year-old retired IAS officer in South Delhi's Hauz Khas Enclave. The blaze, which broke out late Wednesday night, also led to his son being hospitalized. Firefighters quickly contained the flames, and authorities are investigating the incident, with no foul play suspected.

title: Norway Chess: Gukesh loses his last game to Carlsen; Divya's unbeaten streak ends
content: World Champion D Gukesh suffered a 42-move classical defeat against World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen on the eve of his 20th birthday in Round 4 of Norway Chess 2026. Meanwhile, Divya Deshmukh's brilliant unbeaten streak came to an end after losing her Armageddon tiebreak to Ukraine's Anna Muzychuk. R Praggnanandhaa won his game, while Koneru Humpy's tough run continues.

title: ‘To please some Delhi boss’: Wangchuk hits back at LG amid ‘honorary cockroach’ row
content: Recalling the meeting, Wangchuk said he was surprised by the message conveyed through Saxena's post on X after what he described as a friendly and constructive discussion. "I thought this was maybe to please some boss somewhere in Delhi who had instructed him to call me up and say such things, but the meeting was none like this, only the tweet was," he said.

title: Rs 30 crore for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in IPL Auction? Batting legend's record-breaking bid
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 97 off 29 balls, including a record-breaking 12 sixes, propelled Rajasthan Royals to a crucial IPL win.  His remarkable performance and maturity have drawn comparisons to legends, with experts valuing him at Rs 30 crore. Sooryavanshi leads the tournament in runs and strike rate.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for May 29, 2026 - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Varun Beverages (VBL), and Eternal are the top stock picks by Bajaj Broking Research has chosen for May 29, 2026:

title: Oil prices: Crude drops below $95 amid US-Iran ceasefire extension speculation
content: Oil prices dipped Friday as traders reacted to reports of a potential 60-day extension to a US-Iran ceasefire, which could ease restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz. While US Vice President Vance indicated a deal is close, sticking points remain, and renewed tensions on the ground underscore the ceasefire's fragility.

title: Quote of the day by Morgan Freeman
content: The rise of outrage culture and social media's impact have stifled open expression, leading to public shaming over nuanced discussions. This environment fosters fear of repercussions, causing many to self-censor and reducing complex issues to black and white. True progress requires a return to tolerant discourse where disagreement is welcomed.

title: UN to honour 2 Indian peacekeepers posthumously & award Major Abhilasha Barak
content: India's unwavering commitment to UN peacekeeping is highlighted as two brave soldiers, Lance Havildar Harbhajan Singh and Naib Subedar Sujit Kumar Pradhan, will be posthumously honored with the Dag Hammarskjold Medal. Major Abhilasha Barak, a pioneering combat helicopter pilot, also receives the Military Gender Advocate of the Year award for her exceptional work.

title: IMD forecasts heatwave relief in North India; storms, strong winds likely
content: Northwest India is on the brink of a refreshing weather transformation, with a notable cooldown expected in the next three days as heatwave conditions diminish. With temperatures projected to drop by 5-10 degrees Celsius, this is a much-needed reprieve for weary residents.

title: India 'Commits' To Buying $500 Billion Worth Of US Goods. Experts Weigh In
content: The concerns gain significance because India is already struggling to preserve its foreign reserves due to a weakening currency and rising oil prices due to the US-Iran war.

title: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: Siddaramaiah Meets Rahul Gandhi, Mallikarjun Kharge In Delhi
content: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: A change in leadership in the three-year-old Karnataka government appears imminent, with Siddaramaiah having sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, reportedly to tender his resignation.

title: Watch: JD Vance Chest Bumps Air Force Cadet At Graduation Ceremony, Then A 'Note To Self'
content: After his speech, US Vice President JD Vance greeted members of the graduating class when one cadet surprised him with a playful chest bump.

title: India Likely To See Below-Average Monsoon Rains In 2026. Reason: El Nino
content: El Nino, characterised by warmer-than-average sea surface temperatures in the central and eastern Pacific, disrupts the Walker Circulation and often suppresses monsoon activity

title: Weather Today LIVE: India Likely To Witness 90% Of Long Period Average Rainfall This Monsoon
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Strong winds and light rain bring relief from heat in Delhi with thunderstorms overnight.

content: The latest leader to speak up is Trinamool Congress's Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Shekhar Roy, who, among other things, had predicted a disintegration of the party.

title: Ebola Returns To A Broken Congo, This Time With Virus Science Isn't Ready For
content: This is where the Democratic Republic of Congo's latest Ebola outbreak has erupted in the middle of a grinding conflict that has hollowed out public services and displaced millions.

title: Ex-Rajasthan Village Head Living With 2 Wives Murdered. 1st Wife, Children Arrested
content: The victims have been identified as Ram Singh Choudhary, a former sarpanch, his mother Poosi Devi, his second wife Suryagyan Devi, and his niece Mahima Chaudhary.

title: Trump Claims Obama, Biden Spent Millions On Reflecting Pool. They Didn't
content: Donald Trump had criticised his predecessors' efforts to fix the site at a Cabinet meeting on Wednesday, claiming they spent "hundreds of millions of dollars."

title: "She Refused To Pay Drug Dealer": Father Of Indian Student Stabbed In Canada
content: Vidhi Megha, an Indian student, was killed on May 15 in St Catharines in Canada, but her family was informed over 10 days later.

title: Dhanendra Kumar, Ex-IAS Officer And 1st Competition Panel Chief, Dies In AC Blast At Delhi Home
content: While Dhanendra Kumar died during treatment due to smoke inhalation, his son is undergoing treatment and is stated to be out of danger.

title: 22-Year-Old Student From Gujarat Stabbed To Death In Canada
content: The victim has been identified as Vidhi Kalpeshbhai Meghani, a resident of Borsad in the Anand district of Gujarat.

title: $300 Billion Investment, Lebanon Solution: Inside 'Almost There' US-Iran Deal

content: Florida congressman Mike Haridopolos, whose district includes Cape Canaveral, said in a statement on X that he has been in contact with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman regarding the explosion.

content: 5 Killed, Several Feared Trapped After Under-Construction Bridge Collapses In UP's Hamirpur During Storm

title: 8% Of US Lacked Health Insurance In 2025, It May Get Worse: Report
content: Massive changes to Medicaid passed into law last year could result in 10 million more uninsured individuals over a decade.

title: Integrate all emergency helplines into '112' in three months, says SC
content: the suprem court has directed states and UTs to integrate all emergency/ambulance helplines into one helpline . the top court said that the right to trauma care of citizens is an integral part of right to life . a bench of justices also directed them to establish a functional grievance redressal system .

title: 196 days, 7 countries, 23 ports: Cocaine ship’s Brazil-India journey
content: Ashish Chauhan is Assistant Editor with The Times of India, Ahmedabad, with over 15 years of experience in crime, legal, and political reporting. He covers human smuggling, cyber fraud, and caste violence, and has broken major stories on fake IPLs and exam rackets. A former PTI and Gujarat Samachar journalist, he focuses on investigative, impact-driven journalism. Read More

content: Manu Aiyappa has been with The Times of India for over two decades, currently serving as the Political Editor. His reporting focuses on politics, public policy, and in-depth features that aim to inform and engage a broad readership. Over the years, his work has been recognised by both the Press Club of Bangalore and the Karnataka Media Academy for its clarity, credibility, and relevance to public discourse. Read More

title: Viral fame spares 'Donald Trump' buffalo from Eid sacrifice in Bangladesh
content: the 700-kg rare albino buffalo will be "preserved" and "cared for" at the national zoo in Dhaka, after a last-minute intervention by the Bangladesh govt . the buffalo named after the us president - once destined for slaughter on Thursday - will now be 'cared' for . a source said there was a "request" and it was respected by the embassy, a ministry official said .

title: Smaller states beat richest on healthcare spending
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: Cong eyes 'Kerala-style' younger & leaner Karnataka cabinet post-Sidda exit; several seniors may go
content: A Journalist by profession, Sandeep Moudgal has an experience of 18 years in the field with Politics and Policy as forte. He is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Bengaluru bureau and Karnataka as his jurisdiction. Has a Master's degree in Ancient History and Archaeology from Mysore University along with a PGDJ from the Asian College of Journalism. Read More

title: Dharmendra Pradhan defends on-screen marking, owns up to glitches
content: education minister defends 'On-Screen Marking' system as a "progressive instrument" he took responsibility for glitches in the evaluation mechanism that have left students and parents worried . "we are all working on that task," he said after chairing a review meeting .
